creator: thj@FreeBSD.org
text:
Please provide versions of FreeBSD and avrdude and the avrdude command you are using. The output from usbconfig dump_all_desc would help as well, i.e.

sudo usbconfig -d ugen0.3 dump_all_desc

Just as a baseline the random avr programmer I found turned out to be a USBasp clone and it can happily read the flash of the dusty arduino I found. 

This works for me on 14.0-RELEASE and 15-CURRENT (about a week old).

```
$ dmesg 
...
ugen0.3: <www.fischl.de USBasp> at usbus0
$ uname -r
14.0-RELEASE
$ sudo avrdude -p m328p -P usb -c usbasp -U flash:r:flash.bin:r
avrdude error: cannot set sck period; please check for usbasp firmware update
avrdude: AVR device initialized and ready to accept instructions
avrdude: device signature = 0x1e950f (probably m328p)

avrdude: processing -U flash:r:flash.bin:r
avrdude: reading flash memory ...
Reading | ################################################## | 100% 6.29 s 
avrdude: writing output file flash.bin

avrdude done.  Thank you.
```
